facts_disclosed: Raised by nan Iris from Barbados after mum passed age 7, nan is 78

turns.content: quick one right so my nan keeps saying she doesnt want anything for christmas but i know thats not true. what do you get a 78 year old woman who says she has everything she needs

That's a classic grandparent move. Does she have any hobbies or things she really enjoys? Sometimes the best gifts for someone who says they don't want anything are experience-based -- something you do together rather than a physical item.

yeah i mean she loves cooking thats her big thing. like every sunday without fail she makes rice and peas. full caribbean spread you know. shes been doing it since before i was born probably since she came over from barbados in the 60s. its like her thing its not negotiable lol

That's lovely, a tradition that's held for decades. You could lean into that -- maybe a really nice piece of cookware she wouldn't buy herself, or you could put together something personal around that tradition. How close are you two?

bruv she raised me. like she IS my parent you know what i mean. my mum passed when i was 7 and nan just stepped in no questions asked. she was already in her 50s and she just took on a little kid and made it work. i dont think people realise how mad that is you know. she could have said no she could have let the system deal with it but nah she just was like this is my grandson hes mine

That says everything about who she is. She sounds like an incredibly strong woman. And the fact that you clearly recognise what she did and what it cost her -- that bond is something really special. With a gift, I think you want something that honours that relationship specifically, not just a generic present.

yeah exactly thats what i want. like something that says i see you and i know what you did for me. not just a box of chocolates and a card init. she doesnt have much money and she never has but she always made sure i had what i needed. school uniform new boots when i was playing football the lot. nan iris is different bruv shes one of a kind

Iris sounds amazing. Here's an idea -- what about putting together something around her journey? You mentioned she came over from Barbados in the 60s and built this life. Maybe a framed photo collage or a scrapbook that traces her story, with pictures of her, of you growing up, the Sunday cooking tradition. You could include a letter telling her what she means to you. Someone like Iris who gave everything -- hearing it back in your own words would probably mean more than anything you could buy.

ngl that actually got me a bit emotional lol. a letter yeah. because i dont say it enough you know. i tell her i love her and that but i dont think ive ever properly told her like nan you saved my life. because she did. like where would i be without her. i dont even want to think about it tbh

You don't have to think about it because she made sure of that. And trust me, writing it down hits different to saying it in passing. She probably knows how you feel, but seeing it in your words on paper -- that's something she'll keep forever. You could pair it with something small and practical too, maybe something for the kitchen since that's her domain.

yeah like a proper nice dutch pot or something she uses hers till its falling apart because she refuses to replace anything that still works lol. thats such a bajan thing man everything gets used until it literally cannot function anymore. okay so dutch pot plus a letter plus maybe some old photos framed up. thats actually a proper gift init

That's a perfect combination. The dutch pot shows you pay attention to her world, the photos honour her history, and the letter tells her what she means to you. That's three gifts that all say the same thing in different ways -- I see you, I appreciate you, I love you. She's going to cry, fair warning.

oh she will 100% cry and then shell say marcus stop being silly and then shell cook something massive to cover up the emotion because thats what she does haha. alright man this is sorted. thank you genuinely. shes 78 and still going strong and i just want her to know i dont take any of it for granted you know
She knows. But it never hurts to hear it. Happy early Christmas to Iris -- she sounds like she deserves every bit of it. Good luck with the letter, just write from the heart and it'll be perfect.
